title: Research Program for Literacy Without Sound
subtitle: Study design, materials, corpora, and the next empirical steps for signed-language literacy
summary: Third research-facing anchor for the Sutton SignWriting Language and Literacy Research series: a SignWriting-centered program of early studies, materials work, and reusable research infrastructure.
synopsis: This entry turns the pathway model and reading-signed-text agenda into a practical research program. It defines three research lanes: reading and literacy studies, Brazil-grounded empirical work, and corpus, materials, and reusable research infrastructure. The emphasis is on realistic early studies, durable research assets, research ethics, external-validity limits, and disciplined evidence-building rather than script-comparison contests or premature large-scale claims.
